subject: Physics
title: Electrons: The Charge Carriers
description: Learn Electrons: The Charge Carriers in one minute.

hook.text: J.J. Thomson discovered the electron in 1897—the tiny particle that carries all static and current electricity.

buildup.text: Thomson shot cathode rays through magnetic fields and found they bent—meaning they carried charge and mass.

discovery.text: He calculated the charge-to-mass ratio and realized these particles were 1,836 times lighter than hydrogen atoms.

twist.text: Electrons are the reason static cling, lightning, and every electronic device works. One particle runs it all.

climax.text: Thomson won the Nobel Prize in 1906. His discovery opened the door to electronics and modern technology.

punchline.text: A particle 1,836 times lighter than an atom. It powers the modern world.
